national_resources.name: National Cyber Crime Reporting Portal
national_resources.description: Official government portal for filing formal complaints for all cybercrimes.

national_resources.name: National Cyber Crime Helpline (1930)
national_resources.description: Dedicated call center for immediate reporting and freezing of digital financial fraud funds.

national_resources.name: CERT-In (Computer Emergency Response)
national_resources.description: National agency responding to computer security incidents and publishing threat warnings.

national_resources.name: RBI Ombudsman (Financial Disputes)
national_resources.description: Statutory body for resolution of banking and digital wallet disputes (file within 3 days).

national_resources.name: Sanchar Saathi Portal (DoT)
national_resources.description: Official portal to block lost/stolen mobile connections and report duplicate SIM cards.

threat_intelligence.description: Fraudsters pose as central law enforcement (CBI, Customs, Narcotics) over WhatsApp/Skype video calls, claiming you sent illegal packages, and placing you under fake 'digital arrest' to extort money.
threat_intelligence.example_script: "Your Aadhaar card was linked to a narcotics shipment. You must remain on camera for digital arrest. Transfer Rs 3 Lakhs security deposit to avoid imprisonment."
threat_intelligence.defense_step: Hang up immediately. Government agencies never place citizens under digital arrest, demand money over video calls, or conduct trials online.

threat_intelligence.description: Scammers convince victims they are sending them a 'refund' or 'payment' on WhatsApp/OLX and ask the victim to scan a QR code to receive the money, which actually debits the victim's account.
threat_intelligence.example_script: "Scan this QR code to claim your Rs. 5,000 cash back from the online marketplace immediately. Enter your UPI PIN to approve the credit."
threat_intelligence.defense_step: Never scan a QR code or enter your UPI PIN to receive money. Scanning a QR code is exclusively used for making payments, not receiving them.

threat_intelligence.description: Fraudsters contact victims posing as tech support or bank officers and convince them to install screen-sharing applications (AnyDesk, TeamViewer) to fix a bug or verify a transaction, stealing OTPs and bank logins.
threat_intelligence.example_script: "Your mobile banking security certificate has expired. Install the official support app AnyDesk from PlayStore and give us the 9-digit access code."
threat_intelligence.defense_step: Never share access codes or install remote assistance software at the behest of cold callers. Keep your bank credentials and OTP screen strictly private.

threat_intelligence.description: Victims are recruited via Telegram/WhatsApp for simple jobs (liking videos, reviewing products). After earning minor payouts, they are coerced into depositing larger 'investments' or registration fees to withdraw their commissions.
threat_intelligence.example_script: "Earn Rs 5,000 per day by rating YouTube videos. Join our task group. To unlock VIP tasks and withdraw your balance, pay a refundable Rs 15,000 security deposit."
threat_intelligence.defense_step: Ignore unverified Telegram or WhatsApp recruiters. Genuine employers never charge security deposits or demand payments to pay out earned wages.
